title: Design decision: enum vs separate methods for chat_create_*
description:
Record a design decision before implementing. Two options:

Option A — single method with enum input:
  pub enum ChatCreate<'a> { Direct { contact_id }, Group { name, members, ... }, Channel { space_id, name, ... } }
  client.chat_create(&session, &ChatCreate::Direct { contact_id: 'x' })
  Pro: one entry point, exhaustive, matches spec's single Chat/set method
  Con: larger enum type, callers must import variant names

Option B — rename to chat_direct / chat_group / chat_channel:
  Pro: minimal change, clear per-kind
  Con: still not consistent with space_create (no per-kind variants)

Recommendation: Option A if callers will match on the kind at runtime; Option B if callers always know the kind at compile time (TUI use case). For a general library, Option A is more idiomatic — it mirrors how serde enums work.
design: Option A: single chat_create(input: &ChatCreateInput<'a>) with ChatCreateInput as an enum. Matches existing SpaceJoinInput enum pattern in this codebase. Variants: Direct { client_id, contact_id }, Group { client_id, name, member_ids, description, avatar_blob_id, message_expiry_seconds }, Channel { client_id, space_id, name, description }. Remove chat_create_direct, chat_create_group, chat_create_channel methods.
